nameMeanings.name: Koerper
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Koerber
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Lorenz, Otto, Theodor, Bernd, Erwin, Florian, Frieda, Inge, Ingeborg, Kurt.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (Körber): occupational name for a basket maker, from an agent derivative of Middle High German korp, German Korb ‘basket’. Compare Korber .

nameMeanings.name: Michael
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of a common biblical name (meaning ‘who is like God?’ in Hebrew) borne by one of the archangels, the protector of the ancient Hebrews, who is also regarded as a saint of the Catholic Church. In the Middle Ages, Michael was regarded as captain of the heavenly host (see Revelation 12:7–9), symbol of the Church Militant, and patron of soldiers. He was often depicted bearing a flaming sword. The name is also borne by a Persian prince and ally of Belshazzar mentioned in the Book of Daniel. Since the early 1900s it has been one of the most enduringly popular boys' names in the English-speaking world.  See also  Michal .

en.lifeSummary: When Michael Koerper was born in 1582, in Zochenreuth, Aufseß, Bayreuth, Bavaria, Germany, his father, Michael Kerber, was 16 and his mother, Caterina Heimsheimer, was 17. He had at least 1 son with Cunigunda Neuner. He died in 1658, in Buffalo, Erie, New York, United States, at the age of 76.

ko.lifeSummary: Michael Koerper(이)가 1582년 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 베이레우스 오프세ss 즈옥헨레우스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Michael Kerber(은)는 16세였고, 그의 어머니 Caterina Heimsheimer(은)는 17세였다. 그는 Cunigunda Neuner(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1658년 미국 뉴욕 주 에리 버팔로에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.
